title: Reading inserted results
summary: Learn how to read and verify the results of INSERT operations in SQL, including how to check what was added and understand the impact of your statements on the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: How to Check Inserted Data
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
After you run an `INSERT` statement, you can confirm the new row by running a `SELECT` query. For example, if you insert a new item into the `inventory_items` table, you can use:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name = 'New Item';
```

This will show you the row(s) matching the name you just inserted. Checking inserted results helps you verify that your data was added as expected.

sketchBlocks.title: Default Values After Insert
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Some columns in `inventory_items` have default values, such as `status` (default: 'draft') and `last_updated` (default: '2026-02-01'). If you omit these columns in your `INSERT` statement, the database will automatically fill them with their default values. You can see these defaults by selecting the new row after insertion.

quizDraft.title: Insert and Read Back a Row
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new row into the inventory_items table with the following values: name = 'Desk Organizer', category = 'Accessories', price = 13.50. Then, write a SELECT statement to show the row(s) where name is 'Desk Organizer'.
quizDraft.hint: Use INSERT INTO with a column list, then SELECT with a WHERE clause.

help.concept: To confirm an insert, add the row and then query for it using SELECT and a WHERE filter.
help.hint_1: First, insert the row with the required columns. Then, select from inventory_items where name matches.
help.hint_2: Write two statements: one INSERT, one SELECT. The SELECT should filter by name.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Add your INSERT statement here

-- Add your SELECT statement here
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 13.50);
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name = 'Desk Organizer';

quizDraft.title: Check Default Values After Insert
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new row into inventory_items with name = 'Sample Coaster', category = 'Kitchen', price = 3.25. Do not specify status or last_updated. Then, select the row(s) where name is 'Sample Coaster' to see the default values.
quizDraft.hint: Omit status and last_updated in your INSERT. Use SELECT to view the result.

help.concept: If you don't specify columns with defaults, the database fills them in automatically.
help.hint_1: Insert only name, category, and price. Then select by name.
help.hint_2: Check the status and last_updated columns in your SELECT result.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Write your INSERT and SELECT statements
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 3.25);
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name = 'Sample Coaster';

quizDraft.title: Verify Multiple Inserts
quizDraft.prompt: Insert two new rows into inventory_items: (name = 'Mini Poster', category = 'Decor', price = 6.00) and (name = 'Travel Cup', category = 'Kitchen', price = 8.75). Then, select all rows where category is 'Kitchen'.
quizDraft.hint: Use a single INSERT statement for both rows, then SELECT with a WHERE filter.

help.concept: You can insert multiple rows at once and then query to confirm all relevant rows.
help.hint_1: List both sets of values in the INSERT. Use SELECT to filter by category.
help.hint_2: Check that both new and existing 'Kitchen' items appear in your SELECT result.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Write your INSERT and SELECT statements
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Mini Poster', 'Decor', 6.00), ('Travel Cup', 'Kitchen', 8.75);
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Kitchen';

quizDraft.title: What Can You Learn by Selecting After Insert?
quizDraft.prompt: After inserting a new row and running a SELECT query for it, which of the following can you confirm? Choose all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what information is visible in the selected row.

help.concept: Selecting after an insert lets you verify the new row's presence and see how the database filled in any default or auto-generated values.
help.hint_1: You can check both your inserted values and any defaults the database applied.
help.hint_2: You cannot see the exact SQL you ran, but you can see the resulting data.

options: a. That the row was actually added
b. The values of columns you inserted
c. The default values for columns you did not specify
d. The SQL statement you used for the insert

quizDraft.title: Default Value for status
quizDraft.prompt: When you insert a row into inventory_items and do not specify the status column, what value will status have by default?
quizDraft.hint: Check the table definition for the default value of status.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT value in the schema will use that value if not specified in an INSERT.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the inventory_items schema for status.
help.hint_2: The default is a string value set in the table definition.

quizDraft.template: The default value for status is [blank].
